LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ANDRE BENGUEREL'S MOTION TO STRIKE PUNITIVE DAMAGES FROM THE COMPLAINT - CCP 435.5 REQUIRES A "MEET AND CONFER IN PERSON OR BY TELEPHONE" BEFORE A MOTION TO STRIKE MAY BE FILED. COUNSEL'S DECLARATION INDICATES THAT NO SUCH MEET-AND-CONFER HAS OCCURRED HERE, SO THIS MOTION IS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR TO ALLOW COMPLIANCE. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER IMMEDIATELY AND DEFENDANT HAS UNTIL AUGUST 19, 2019 TO RESPOND TO THE COMPLAINT. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR#6185, TONYVAUGHN@YAHOO.COM, REPORTED.

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-11-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. SCOTT RIGHTHAND, ESQ. of Law Office of Scott Righthand (Tel: 415-544-0115) appearing for plaintiffs;
Atty. ALICIA L. QUEEN, ESQ. of Hartsuyker, Stratman & Williams-Abrego (Tel: 510-457-3440) appearing for defendant Andrew Benguerel.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT ANDRE BENGUEREL MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with the modification of removing language pertaining to a demurrer being sustained: Defendant Andre Benguerel's motion to strike punitive damages from plaintiffs' complaint is granted. In order to state a prima facie claim for punitive damages, a complaint must set forth the elements stated in the general punitive damage statute, Civil Code section 3294, including allegations that the defendant has been guilty of oppression, fraud, or malice. (Civ. Code 3294(a).) "Malice" is defined as conduct "intended by the defendant to cause injury to plaintiff, or despicable conduct that is carried on by the defendant with a willful and conscious disregard for the rights or safety of others." (Civ. Code 3294(c)(1).) Absent an intent to injure the plaintiff, malice requires more than a "willful and conscious" disregard of the plaintiff's interests; the additional component of "despicable conduct" must be found. (College Hospital, Inc. v. Superior Court (1994) 8 Cal.4th 704, 725.) Punitive damages are appropriate if the defendant's actions are reprehensible, fraudulent, or in blatant violation of law or policy. The mere carelessness or ignorance of the defendant does not justify the imposition of punitive damages. Punitive damages are proper only when the tortious conduct rises to levels of extreme indifference to the plaintiff's rights, a level which decent citizens should not have to tolerate. (Pacific Gas and Electric Company v. Superior Court (2018) 24 Cal.App.5th 1150, 1170-76.) The allegations in the complaint (Complaint, Para. 4) that defendant was skiing downhill "at an unreasonably high rate of speed in a reckless and car[e]less manner" in an area intended for beginning skiers may amount to recklessness but do not rise to the level of despicable conduct carried on by the defendant with a willful and conscious disregard for the rights or safety of others required by Civ. Code 3294(c)(1). (See Lackner v. North (2006) 135 Cal.App.4th 1188, 1209-10 [trial court properly granted motion for summary adjudication of punitive damages where no reasonable juror could find that snowboarder's conduct was despicable; even if snowboarder was reckless in traveling at high speed in rest area without looking in the direction he was heading, he did not hit skier intentionally and attempted to avoid her]; Brousseau v. Jarrett (1977) 73 Cal.App.3d 864, 872 ["conclusory characterization of defendant's conduct as intentional, willful and fraudulent is a patently insufficient statement of 'oppression, fraud, or malice, express or implied,' within the meaning of section 3294."].) For these reasons, the Court strikes from the complaint the prayer for punitive and exemplary damages, together with the sentence "Defendants [sic] actions were with malice, with knowledge of the injury he was about to cause, in conscious disregard for the safety of Plaintiff." (Complaint, Para. 4.) Plaintiffs have not shown how amendment could cure the defect in the complaint. If plaintiffs develop additional facts in discovery that support a claim for punitive damages, they may file a motion for leave to amend the complaint.

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Patty Dowling, CSR# 5388, e-mail: pdowling@sftc.org (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion To Strike Punitive Damages From The Complaint
Off Calendar Jul-19-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ANDRE BENGUEREL's Motion To Strike Punitive Damages From The Complaint.

CCP 435.5 requires a "meet and confer in person or by telephone" before a motion to strike may be filed. Counsel's declaration indicates that no such meet-and-confer has occurred here, so this motion is ordered OFF CALENDAR to allow compliance. The parties are ordered to meet and confer immediately and defendant has until August 19, 2019 to respond to the complaint.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
